title: Haseena J. Enu | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP

A seasoned Fragomen leader, Haseena’s career spans multiple leadership roles, equipping her with a broad and insightful perspective on immigration law and client success. Currently serving as the managing partner of Fragomen’s Dallas office, she previously led the firm’s Washington, DC office and, before that, managed a bespoke practice in New York, representing small and emerging companies, investors, entrepreneurs and other individuals.
Her client-focused approach has fostered long-standing relationships—many of her clients continue to seek her guidance as they navigate career moves across organizations and industries. She works with businesses of all sizes across a broad range of sectors, including telecommunications, semiconductors, infrastructure, construction, oil and gas, green energy, manufacturing, food and beverage and technology.
Known for her strategic insight, Haseena helps clients align their immigration policies with business objectives, considering factors such as risk tolerance, talent acquisition, retention needs and communication preferences. She doesn’t just manage immigration programs—she continuously enhances them, leveraging Fragomen’s industry-leading benchmarking and technological capabilities to drive effective outcomes.
A dedicated advocate for pro bono work, Haseena serves as a mentor for the American Bar Association’s Afghan Legal Professionals Scholarship and Mentoring Pilot Program, assisting Afghan refugees and permanent residents with legal training and accreditation in the US. She is currently supporting one participant in law school with course selection, study recommendations and schedule management.
Additionally, she provides pro bono representation for non-citizen and non-resident students in the New York-based Prep for Prep program, which prepares exceptional minority students for private secondary and post-secondary education. She also works with Catholic Charities to assist immigrants with their citizenship applications.

She also works with Catholic Charities to assist immigrants with their citizenship applications.
- Cornell University Law School, J.D., 1994
- Columbia University, School of International and Public Affairs
- Brown University, B.A., 1990
- American University in Cairo, Egypt
Bar Admissions: New York
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Listed in Best Lawyers in America (Woodward/White, Inc.), 2025 to 2026
Listed in Chambers USA (Chambers and Partners) , 2022 to 2025
Listed in Texas Lawyer ’s Texas Legal Awards Best Mentors (ALM Media Properties, LLC), 2022

### Professional accomplishments
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association (AILA)
Member, New York State Bar Association
Member, Dallas and American Bar Associations
Council Member, Dallas Bar Association, International Law Section
Planning Committee, University of Texas School of Law Annual Corporate Counsel Institute
Member, Brown University, Women’s Leadership Counsel
Represented a joint venture formed from the spin-off of divisions of two major multinational corporations headquartered outside the United States, performing due diligence and advising on immigration aspects of the transaction, which impacted more than 500 foreign nationals working in the United States

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “Immigration Breakfast Briefing.” Fragomen , Dallas, TX, 13 November 2025.
Speaker, “Fragomen Semiconductor Industry Group – Fueling Innovation: U.S. and Canadian Immigration Strategies for Semiconductor Start-Ups.” Fragomen , 18 June 2025.
Speaker, “Expert Insight: Navigating U.S. Immigration Uncertainties: Strategies for the Global Workforce.” 2025 Forum for Expatriate Management Americas Summit , Dallas, TX, 14 May 2025.
Speaker, “Opening Panel Discussion: Are We Seeing a New World Order? What Would that Mean for Mobility?” 2025 Forum for Expatriate Management Americas Summit , Dallas, TX, 14 May 2025.
Interviewed, “ Q&A with Haseena Enu: A ‘Firm’ Commitment to Social Responsibility .” American Bar Association’s Afghan Legal Professionals Scholarship & Mentoring Pilot Program Newsletter , Volume 3, April 2025.
Speaker, “Business Immigration and Compliance: Key Updates and What Lies Ahead.” Fragomen , Webinar, 25 February 2025.
Speaker, “The Strategic Proposition for HR: Mergers and Acquisitions - Challenges and Best Practices for Successful Integration.” ERC Conference , Grapevine, Texas, May 2018.
Speaker, “A Beginner’s Guide to Managing Global Assignment: Immigration Considerations.” The Forum for Expatriate Management, Southwest Texas Chapter Meeting , San Antonio, Texas, August 16, 2016.
Co-panelist, “Update on Global Corporate Immigration Trends.” Dallas Fort Worth International HR Roundtable , April 16, 2014.
Co-panelist, “Crew Staffing Challenges for Offshore Support Vessels in Territorial & Outer Continental Shelf Waters.” WorkBoat.com Webinar , April 24, 2013.
Speaker, “Developing Global Immigration Policies.” Totally Expat Southwest 2013 , Houston, Texas, February 25, 2013.
Speaker, “Meet the Experts Immigration Panel.” Totally Expat Southwest 2013 , Houston, Texas, February 25, 2013.
Speaker, “Business Immigration 2012: Mobility Trends, Legal Outlook and Legislative Trends.” Global HR News Conference: Making Mobility More Effective , Houston, Texas, November 1, 2012.
Speaker, “Global Immigration Policies.” Forum for Expatriate Management Dallas/Ft. Worth Chapter Meeting , Dallas, Texas, August 22, 2012.
Speaker, “U.S. Business Immigration: Hot Topics in 2009.” HR Southwest Conference , Ft. Worth, Texas, October 2009.
Speaker, “Immigration Essentials.” American Council on International Personnel, 2009 Annual Symposium , Washington, DC, June 2009.
Author, “Visa, Travel and Work-Site Enforcement.” Texas Lawyer , Vol. 25, No. 12, June 2009.
Speaker, “Business Immigration: Trends in Enforcement and Compliance.” 17th Annual Gulf Coast Symposium on Human Resource Issues , Houston, Texas, May 2008.
Speaker, “Business Immigration Law Update.” Dallas Bar Association , Dallas, Texas, April 2008.
Speaker, “U.S. Business Immigration: Requirements in 2008.” Texas State Council of SHRM, 7th Annual International HR Conference , Houston, Texas, January 2008.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
